question_text: Two runners complete a 400 m race. Runner A finishes in 50 seconds. Runner B's distance-time graph shows she travels at a constant speed of 9 m/s. Which runner is faster, and by how much (in m/s)?
correct_answer: Runner B is faster by 1 m/s (Runner A: 8 m/s; Runner B: 9 m/s; difference: 1 m/s)
explanation: Calculate Runner A's speed: 400 m ÷ 50 s = 8 m/s. Runner B's speed is given as 9 m/s from the graph. The difference is 9 - 8 = 1 m/s, so Runner B is faster. This requires you to calculate one speed and compare it with information from a graph.
